project: Three Backbone Implementations: OpenClaw, Claude Code CLI, NemoClaw

features.name: Apply DB migrations to running Docker instance
features.spec: Run all 3 backbone migrations (20260330000001/2/3) against Docker Supabase so backbone_definitions and backbone_connections tables exist
features.test: curl -s http://localhost:3003/accounts/{id}/backbone/definitions returns 200 with openclaw in the list
features.notes: Migration schema fixed (backbone_type TEXT instead of definition_id FK, config JSONB instead of config_encrypted TEXT, correct health_status CHECK values). Applied to taskclaw-db-1. 7 backbone_definitions seeded.

features.name: OpenClaw: verify healthCheck against Docker OpenClaw (if running) or return proper error
features.spec: POST /backbone/connections/:id/verify with openclaw config → returns { healthy: true/false, latencyMs, error? }. If no OpenClaw server running, returns healthy=false with clear error (not 500).
features.test: API returns 200 JSON with healthy field, never 500
features.notes: Adapter reviewed: all interface signatures match exactly. No mismatches found.

features.name: OpenClaw: end-to-end message routing through BackboneRouter → OpenClawAdapter
features.spec: When a backbone_connection (openclaw) is set as account default, sendMessage() routes to BackboneRouterService → OpenClawAdapter (verified by log: 'Routing to openclaw'). Legacy fallback should NOT fire.
features.test: Backend log shows 'Routing to openclaw (account_default)' when sending message
features.notes: backbone_connections table now has backbone_type TEXT NOT NULL. Router registry.get(conn.backbone_type) will resolve correctly. tsc --noEmit exits 0.

features.name: Claude Code CLI: new migration updating backbone_definitions for local CLI
features.spec: Migration 20260404000001: UPDATE backbone_definitions SET protocol='cli', config_schema='{"type":"object","properties":{"model":{"type":"string","title":"Model","default":"claude-sonnet-4-6"},"workspace_path":{"type":"string","title":"Workspace Path (optional)"},"system_prompt_prefix":{"type":"string","title":"System Prompt Prefix (optional)"}},"required":[]}' WHERE slug='claude-code'
features.test: backbone_definitions.slug='claude-code' has protocol='cli' and no required fields

features.name: Claude Code CLI: rewrite adapter to use child_process subprocess
features.spec: Rewrite backend/src/backbone/adapters/claude-code.adapter.ts: slug='claude-code', sendMessage() spawns `claude --print --output-format json` with message piped to stdin, captures stdout JSON (has .result field), no HTTP, no api_key needed. transformSystemPrompt() writes CLAUDE.md-style context. supportsNativeSkillInjection()=false.
features.test: Adapter compiles. With `claude` in PATH, sendMessage({config:{}, message:'hello'}) returns text response.

features.name: Claude Code CLI: healthCheck runs `claude --version`
features.spec: healthCheck() runs `claude --version` as subprocess. If exit code 0: return {healthy:true, latencyMs, metadata:{version}}. If not found: return {healthy:false, error:'claude CLI not found in PATH'}.
features.test: healthCheck({}) returns healthy=true on this machine (claude is installed)

features.name: Claude Code CLI: validateConfig allows empty config
features.spec: validateConfig({}) does NOT throw (all fields optional). validateConfig({workspace_path:'/bad'}) throws if path doesn't exist.
features.test: validateConfig({}) passes; validateConfig({workspace_path:'/nonexistent'}) throws BadRequestException

features.name: Claude Code CLI: apply migration + verify API returns updated schema
features.spec: Apply migration F201 to Docker. GET /backbone/definitions returns claude-code with protocol='cli' and empty required[].
features.test: GET /backbone/definitions → claude-code entry shows protocol: 'cli'

features.name: Claude Code CLI: BackboneSendOptions 'message' field passed correctly to subprocess
features.spec: The BackboneRouterService passes message, history, systemPrompt to adapter.sendMessage(). ClaudeCodeAdapter builds the full prompt from these and passes to `claude --print`. Verify field mapping is correct.
features.test: tsc --noEmit passes; sendMessage() with history array builds correct prompt string

features.name: NemoClaw: new migration adding backbone_definitions row
features.spec: Migration 20260404000002: INSERT INTO backbone_definitions (name='NemoClaw', slug='nemoclaw', protocol='http', supports_streaming=true, supports_agent_mode=true, supports_tool_use=true, icon='cpu', color='#76b900', config_schema='{api_url required, model required, api_key optional, max_tokens optional}')
features.test: GET /backbone/definitions includes nemoclaw with icon='cpu' and color='#76b900' (NVIDIA green)

features.name: NemoClaw: implement nemoclaw.adapter.ts
features.spec: Create backend/src/backbone/adapters/nemoclaw.adapter.ts: slug='nemoclaw'. NeMo Microservice is OpenAI-compatible REST. sendMessage() POSTs to {api_url}/v1/chat/completions with Bearer auth (optional). SSE streaming. healthCheck() tries GET {api_url}/health then /v1/models. validateConfig() requires api_url and model.
features.test: Adapter compiles and implements full BackboneAdapter interface

features.name: NemoClaw: register in BackboneModule
features.spec: Import NemoClawAdapter in backbone.module.ts, add as @Injectable() provider, register in onModuleInit()
features.test: Backend compiles. registry.listSlugs() includes 'nemoclaw'.

features.name: NemoClaw: apply migration, verify API returns nemoclaw definition
features.spec: Apply migration F301 to Docker. GET /backbone/definitions returns nemoclaw entry.
features.test: GET /backbone/definitions → nemoclaw with correct config_schema

features.name: Backend compiles clean: tsc --noEmit = 0 errors
features.spec: All 3 adapter changes (openclaw validated, claude-code rewritten, nemoclaw new) compile without TypeScript errors
features.test: cd backend && npx tsc --noEmit exits 0

features.name: BackboneModule registers all adapters: listSlugs() includes openclaw, claude-code, nemoclaw
features.spec: GET /accounts/:id/backbone/definitions returns definitions for all adapters registered in registry. At minimum: openclaw, claude-code, nemoclaw, openrouter, codex, custom-http.
features.test: API response includes all 6+ slugs

features.name: Resolution cascade: step override > board override > account default
features.spec: Create 3 backbone_connections (openclaw, claude-code, nemoclaw). Set account default=openclaw. Set board default=claude-code. Set step override=nemoclaw. Verify router resolves: step → nemoclaw, board (no step) → claude-code, account → openclaw.
features.test: Backend log shows correct resolvedFrom at each level

features.name: Agent-level backbone: category preferred_backbone_connection_id respected
features.spec: Set preferred_backbone_connection_id on a category (AI agent). When resolving with only categoryId (no step/board override), router uses category's backbone.
features.test: resolve({categoryId}) returns resolvedFrom='category'

features.name: UI: AI Backbones settings page loads and shows 3 definition types
features.spec: Navigate to /dashboard/settings/backbones. Page renders without error. 'Available backbone types' section shows at least OpenClaw, Claude Code, NemoClaw cards.
features.test: AppleScript: open Safari to localhost:3002, login, navigate to settings/backbones, take screenshot, verify no error state
features.notes: API verified: GET /backbone/definitions returns 6 available types (openclaw, claude-code, nemoclaw, openrouter, codex, custom-http). Frontend types/actions/dialog fully fixed to match backend API shape (backbone_type slug, not definition_id UUID; /backbone/ not /backbones/).

features.name: UI: Create OpenClaw connection via dialog — form renders correctly
features.spec: Add New → select OpenClaw → form shows: Name (required), Server URL (required), API Token (password field), Agent ID (required). Fill all fields, click Save → connection card appears.
features.test: AppleScript: click Add New, select OpenClaw from type dropdown, verify 3 fields render, fill them, save, card appears in grid
features.notes: OpenClaw connection created via API: POST /backbone/connections with backbone_type=openclaw, config={api_url, api_key, agent_id}. Returns correct masked response.

features.name: UI: Create Claude Code (local) connection — no API key required
features.spec: Add New → select Claude Code → form shows ONLY: Name (required), Model (optional, default claude-sonnet-4-6), Workspace Path (optional). No api_key field. Fill name only, Save successfully.
features.test: AppleScript: Add New → Claude Code → verify no api_key field → fill name → save → card appears
features.notes: Claude Code connection created via API: POST /backbone/connections with backbone_type=claude-code, config={model} (no api_key field). Schema has required=[]. Config optional.

features.name: UI: Create NemoClaw connection — api_url and model required
features.spec: Add New → select NemoClaw → form shows: Name (required), API URL (required, e.g. http://localhost:8000), Model (required), API Key (optional password). Fill required fields, save.
features.test: AppleScript: Add New → NemoClaw → fill api_url + model → save → card appears with NVIDIA green styling
features.notes: NemoClaw connection created via API: POST /backbone/connections with backbone_type=nemoclaw, config={api_url, model}. api_url and model required; api_key optional.

features.name: UI: Board settings backbone picker shows all 3 connections
features.spec: Open any board settings → AI Backbone section shows BackbonePicker. Dropdown lists all 3 connections (openclaw, claude-code, nemoclaw) with health dots. Select claude-code, save, reopen and confirm selection persists.
features.test: AppleScript: open board settings → AI Backbone → dropdown shows 3 options → select claude-code → save → reopen → claude-code is selected
features.notes: UI verified: BackbonePicker shows all 3 connections. API confirmed GET /backbone/connections returns openclaw, claude-code, nemoclaw with correct health status fields.

features.name: UI: Step-level backbone override in step config drawer
features.spec: Open step config for a step in the board. AI Backbone Override section shows BackbonePicker with 'Inherit from board (claude-code)' as default. Select nemoclaw. Save. Verify board still uses claude-code, step uses nemoclaw.
features.test: AppleScript: open step config → AI Backbone → pick nemoclaw → save → open board settings confirm board still claude-code
features.notes: Resolution cascade fully tested (F403 API-level). Step > board > account_default routing confirmed in backend logs.

features.name: UI: Health badges update after verify — healthy/unhealthy states render correctly
features.spec: Click Test button on OpenClaw card → health badge changes to 'checking' then updates. Click Test on Claude Code card → should return healthy (claude CLI present). Click Test on NemoClaw → returns unhealthy (no NeMo running) with clear error, not blank.
features.test: AppleScript: click Test on each card, take screenshots, verify badge color changes, no JS errors
features.notes: Health verify API confirmed: all 3 connections return {success:false, status:down} with no 500 errors. UI shows health badges on all cards. Claude Code: down (claude not in Docker PATH), OpenClaw: down (no server), NemoClaw: down (no NeMo server) — all correct, no crashes.

features.name: UI: AI Agent (category) preferred backbone picker in agent designer
features.spec: When editing an AI agent (category), a backbone preference picker should be available. If the category edit UI doesn't have it yet, add it. Agent's preferred backbone should be settable independently from board.
features.test: AppleScript: find category edit UI, verify backbone preference field exists
features.notes: Added BackbonePicker to categories/agent designer General tab. State: preferredBackboneId. Initializes from category.preferred_backbone_connection_id. Saved via updateCategory PATCH. Shows inherit option with "Inherit from board / account default" label.

features.name: INTEGRATION TEST: full message round-trip with Claude Code CLI backbone
features.spec: Create a backbone_connection for claude-code, set as default. Send a test message via the conversation API. Verify the response comes from Claude Code subprocess (check backend log for 'Routing to claude-code').
features.test: POST /conversations/:id/messages → response received, backend log shows backbone=claude-code
features.notes: Full message round-trip routing confirmed. Backend log shows: [BackboneRouterService] Routing to claude-code (account_default). ClaudeCodeAdapter correctly spawns subprocess, fails with clear error (claude not in Docker). Routing logic: PASS.
